research_basis: OpenAI Agents SDK separates orchestration, handoffs, guardrails, state, and observability when the application owns tool execution and approvals.
OpenAI Agents SDK tracing records agent runs, tool calls, handoffs, guardrails, and custom events for review.
LangChain/LangGraph multi-agent patterns support main-agent subagents, handoffs, skills, routers, and custom workflows with centralized routing tradeoffs.
AutoGen frames multi-agent systems as customizable conversable agents with human input, tools, and programmable interaction patterns.
Recent traceability/accountability research motivates structured roles, handoffs, saved records, and replayable accountability in role-specialized agent pipelines.

scenario_results.notes: Mission trace shows Orchestrator plan, specialist delegation, missing-authority handling, synthesis, and after-action review.
Agent activity view exposes specialist roles when useful without making daily users manage every specialist.
Role Contract defines purpose, tools, forbidden actions, memory scope, evidence, escalation, model policy, rubric, and audit expectations, and enforcement denies direct mutation.
Daily-user role card stays simple while operator view exposes full contract.
Specialist direct mutation attempt is denied and requires governed product/workflow paths.
Orchestrator delegation includes specialist rationale, handled evidence/tool refs, and influence on final outcome.
Specialist outputs are evidence-labeled or explicitly gap-labeled.
Trace and audit link granted autonomy to namespace owner, allowed work, events, and correction/rollback visibility.
Provider switch changes only inference brain; contract hash, tools, memory scope, evidence rules, and audit expectations remain stable.
Agent Role Contract update records versioned diff, impact, rollout guidance, affected missions, and audit.
Prompt-only authority expansion cannot add tools, connector access, memory scope, write permission, or action authority.
Specialist failure records first failing layer, impact, retry/escalation path, continuation state, and user-facing error.
Agent Pack supplied agent can use only explicitly activated workspace capability grants through ConnectorHub-mediated boundaries.
Replay preserves trace, role contracts, provider records, tool outputs, diagnosis refs, judge results, evidence refs, and audit refs without hidden chain-of-thought.

roles.purpose: Own mission planning, delegation, synthesis, authority gaps, and after-action review.
Review artifacts, search snapshots, and evidence bundles without mutating truth.
Draft memory candidates from evidence while leaving approval to governed memory paths.
Prepare Workflow/Action proposals, dry-runs, and rollback-aware execution plans.
Evaluate ambiguous outputs as a supporting signal without mutating memory or rules.
Request ConnectorHub-mediated capabilities and never hold credentials or provider clients.
Evaluate scope, risk, approval, egress, and authority boundaries.
Suggest reusable playbooks from approved experience without auto-globalizing behavior.
